mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate level inversely determines equity discount rates. A higher FEDFUNDS value directly increases the cost of capital for S&P 500 constituents, producing negative forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant negative correlation with FEDFUNDS levels (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in the unemployment rate above 5.0 percent signals a structural labor market deterioration. This threshold crossing triggers immediate sell-offs in cyclical equity sectors, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the threshold crossing fails to generate a significant directional edge (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in real GDP and retail sales indicate a broad-based contraction in aggregate demand. This joint deterioration forces corporate earnings downgrades, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint decline condition yields a positive 60-day forward return in > 60% of cases, or the interaction term fails to improve out-of-sample predictive accuracy over single-series models (AIC delta > 0).

mechanism_narrative: The industrial production index captures real-time manufacturing and utility output. During a post-shock recovery regime, rising INDPRO values confirm supply chain normalization and capacity utilization, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for INDPRO drops below -0.01 during identified post-shock recovery windows, or 5-day forward returns are not significantly positive when INDPRO increases (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Core inflation measured by CPIAUCSL exceeding 3.0 percent forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy. This threshold crossing sustains high discount rates and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 20-day returns in > 70% of instances when CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.0, or the inflation threshold shows no predictive power for equity direction (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: Total nonfarm payrolls represent the primary driver of consumer spending capacity. A higher PAYEMS level guarantees robust household income growth, which directly translates to positive corporate revenue trajectories and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with PAYEMS levels (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between housing starts and auto sales signals a structural shift in consumer demand allocation. When HOUST declines while RSAFS rises, capital flows into durable goods sectors, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The HOUST-RSAFS divergence condition yields negative 5-day forward returns in > 65% of cases, or the interaction effect fails to generate a statistically significant directional edge (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production levels interact with monetary policy to determine equity valuations. During a monetary tightening cycle, high INDPRO values amplify inflationary pressures, forcing further rate hikes that produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for the INDPRO-FEDFUNDS interaction term remains below 0.01 during tightening regimes, or 20-day forward returns are not significantly negative when both series are elevated (p > 0.05).
